_metadata.purpose: Round 2b-full — all 297 non-severe sub-rules across File 3
_metadata.authoring_rule: Work target-by-target. Write one template per target, then substitute ingredient name per sub-rule. Most targets have repeatable mechanism (bleeding = 99 sub-rules under 5 targets).

drafts._source_mechanism: CBD inhibits CYP2C9, which metabolizes warfarin. Case reports document elevated INR when CBD is added to warfarin therapy. The interaction may increase bleeding risk.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR more frequently when starting or stopping CBD. Inform prescriber.

fields_to_author.alert_body: CBD has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding CBD and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: CBD can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Cat's claw procyanidins (B2, C1) directly inhibit thrombin, a key enzyme in the coagulation cascade that converts fibrinogen to fibrin. Combined use with anticoagulants (warfarin, heparins, direct oral anticoagulants) may produce additive anticoagulant effects. The herb is documented in the Heck et al. 2000 AJHP review as a herb with potential warfarin interaction.
drafts._source_action: Inform your prescriber if taking cat's claw while on anticoagulant therapy. Monitor for signs of excessive anticoagulation (unusual bruising, prolonged bleeding). Avoid in patients requiring tight INR control.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Cat's claw has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding cat's claw and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Cat's claw can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Chamomile contains natural coumarins (herniarin, umbelliferone) that may have additive anticoagulant effect with warfarin. A systematic review of 149 herb-warfarin interaction reports specifically identifies chamomile tea as causing warfarin potentiation with bleeding events. The 2000 AJHP review (Heck et al.) lists chamomile among herbs that may potentially increase bleeding or potentiate warfarin effects.
drafts._source_action: Avoid large amounts of chamomile tea (>4 cups/day) or concentrated chamomile extracts while on warfarin. Inform your prescriber. Monitor INR if chamomile consumption is substantial or newly started.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Chamomile has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding chamomile and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Chamomile can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Cranberry may inhibit CYP2C9, the primary isoenzyme responsible for S-warfarin metabolism, theoretically increasing warfarin plasma levels and anticoagulant effect. Case reports from the UK raised concern over INR elevation and serious bleeding in patients taking warfarin with cranberry juice. A systematic review of herb-warfarin interactions identifies cranberry as warranting monitoring in warfarin patients. Two RCTs using CYP2C9 metabolic surrogates did not find significant CYP2C9 inhibition, but these studies used moderate cranberry juice volumes and may not capture concentrated supplement doses.
drafts._source_action: Inform your prescriber of regular cranberry juice or supplement use while on warfarin. Monitor INR with concentrated cranberry supplements. Large volumes of cranberry juice (>1 L/day) or high-dose supplements represent higher risk.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Cranberry has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding cranberry and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Cranberry can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Curcumin may enhance warfarin effect through additive antiplatelet activity (TXA2 suppression, COX-1 inhibition, 12-LOX inhibition) and potential CYP2C9 modulation affecting S-warfarin metabolism. The concentrated extract form poses higher interaction risk than dietary turmeric. Warfarin potentiation by turmeric/curcumin is documented in herb-drug interaction systematic reviews.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR when initiating or escalating curcumin supplementation during warfarin therapy. Inform prescriber. Consider temporary INR check 1-2 weeks after starting curcumin extract.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Curcumin has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding curcumin and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Curcumin can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Devil's claw is listed among herbal products with documented reports of potential warfarin interaction in the AJHP systematic review (Heck et al., 2000), which covers 20 alternative therapy agents with established or theoretical warfarin interactions. The mechanism may involve COX/LOX pathway modulation by harpagoside, affecting platelet function and potentially coagulation factor activity.
drafts._source_action: Inform your prescriber if taking devil's claw while on warfarin or other anticoagulants. Monitor INR if starting or stopping devil's claw supplementation.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Devil's claw has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding devil's claw and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Devil's claw can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: EPO's GLA-derived PGE1 inhibits platelet aggregation and EPO has demonstrated anti-coagulant effects on multiple coagulation parameters (PT, aPTT, thrombin time) in animal studies. Combined with warfarin, additive anticoagulation could result. Listed among herbs with potential warfarin potentiation in clinical pharmacology references.
drafts._source_action: Monitor coagulation status if taking EPO with warfarin or other anticoagulants. Inform prescriber of EPO supplementation.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Evening primrose oil has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding evening primrose oil and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Evening primrose oil can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Fish oil (EPA+DHA) may enhance warfarin's anticoagulant effect via additive antiplatelet activity and possible reduction of factor VII. Multiple case reports document INR elevation (to >4 or 8) when fish oil was added or dose-escalated during stable warfarin therapy. One fatal case involved warfarin-induced coagulopathy that could not be reversed after head trauma in a patient also taking fish oil.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR more frequently when starting, stopping, or changing fish oil dose during warfarin therapy. Inform prescriber. At doses >2 g/day, closer INR surveillance is warranted.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Fish oil has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding fish oil and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Fish oil can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Potential additive anticoagulant/antiplatelet effects.
drafts._source_action: Use only with clinician monitoring for bleeding signs.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Garlic has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding garlic and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Garlic can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Case reports documented reduced warfarin INR with concurrent ginseng use, possibly via CYP enzyme induction.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR closely if combining with warfarin. Inform prescriber.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Ginseng has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding ginseng and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Ginseng can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Multiple case reports and pharmacovigilance data document glucosamine-associated INR elevation in patients on stable warfarin therapy. A systematic pharmacovigilance review (Knudsen & Sokol, 2008) identified 20 FDA MedWatch reports and 21 WHO reports of altered coagulation (increased INR, bleeding) with glucosamine and warfarin, including one case of intraventricular bleed and subdural hematoma. A dose-response relationship was observed: one patient's INR rose from 2.3 to 3.9 after dose escalation from 1000 to 3000 mg/day, then to 4.7 on further dose reduction delay, normalizing after glucosamine discontinuation. The mechanism is unknown — glucosamine is not a known inhibitor of CYP2C9 or CYP3A4.
drafts._source_action: Inform your prescriber if taking glucosamine while on warfarin. Check INR within 2-4 weeks of starting or stopping glucosamine. Dose escalation of glucosamine may destabilize previously stable anticoagulation control.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Glucosamine has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding glucosamine and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Glucosamine can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Omega-3 fatty acids may enhance warfarin's anticoagulant effect through two mechanisms: (1) additive antiplatelet activity via TXA2 pathway suppression, and (2) possible reduction in vitamin K-dependent coagulation factor VII levels. Multiple case reports document INR elevation (from therapeutic range to >4) when fish oil dose was doubled or added to stable warfarin therapy. One case documented INR of 8.06 after initiating fish oil with warfarin.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR more frequently if starting, stopping, or changing the dose of fish oil while on warfarin. Inform your prescriber of fish oil supplementation. At doses >2 g/day, consider closer INR surveillance.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Omega-3 has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding Omega-3 and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Omega-3 can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Case reports and mechanistic data suggest that L-carnitine may enhance the anticoagulant effect of acenocoumarol and potentially warfarin, possibly through altered vitamin K metabolism or carnitine-mediated changes in fatty acid oxidation that affect coagulation factor synthesis.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR when starting or stopping ALCAR in patients on anticoagulants. Alert prescriber to use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Acetyl-L-carnitine has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding Acetyl-L-carnitine and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Acetyl-L-carnitine can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Andrographolide's PAF antagonism and inhibition of thromboxane A2 synthesis may produce additive anticoagulant or antiplatelet effects when combined with warfarin, heparin, or direct oral anticoagulants. No human pharmacokinetic studies of direct andrographis-warfarin interaction are available, but mechanistic evidence supports caution.
drafts._source_action: Monitor for signs of excessive anticoagulation (bruising, prolonged bleeding) if combining andrographis with anticoagulants. INR monitoring is advisable in warfarin users.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Andrographis has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding andrographis and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Andrographis can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Thymoquinone inhibits thromboxane B2 synthesis and platelet aggregation, and in vitro data suggest weak inhibition of CYP2C9 which is the primary metabolic pathway for warfarin S-enantiomer. A combined antiplatelet and potential pharmacokinetic interaction may increase bleeding risk.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR if combining black seed oil with warfarin or other anticoagulants. Alert prescriber to use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Black seed oil has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding black seed oil and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Black seed oil can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Chondroitin sulfate shares structural similarities with heparin (both are sulfated glycosaminoglycans), and case reports have documented clinically significant INR elevation in warfarin-treated patients taking chondroitin-glucosamine combination products. The exact mechanism may involve inhibition of vitamin K-dependent clotting factors or direct potentiation of antithrombin III activity. One case report documented INR increase from 2.6 to 4.1 associated with glucosamine-chondroitin supplementation.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR in warfarin-treated patients who initiate chondroitin supplements. Alert prescriber to supplement use. Consider avoiding chondroitin if INR is already at the upper therapeutic range.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Chondroitin has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding chondroitin and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Chondroitin can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: CoQ10 may have a structural similarity to vitamin K and affect the coagulation cascade. Longitudinal cohort study found CoQ10 users on warfarin had OR 3.69 for self-reported bleeding. A small RCT found no significant INR change, suggesting the interaction may be patient-specific.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR and bleeding signs when initiating or changing CoQ10 dose in patients on warfarin.

fields_to_author.alert_body: CoQ10 has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding CoQ10 and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: CoQ10 can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Cordyceps has demonstrated in vitro platelet aggregation inhibitory activity and adenosine-mediated vasodilation. Cordycepin (adenosine analogue) may contribute to antiplatelet effects additive with anticoagulant therapy.
drafts._source_action: Monitor coagulation parameters when combining cordyceps with anticoagulants. Alert prescriber to supplement use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Cordyceps has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding cordyceps and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Cordyceps can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: DHA reduces platelet TXA2 synthesis and may weakly inhibit vitamin K-dependent coagulation factor synthesis. Combined with warfarin, a small but clinically relevant INR elevation has been observed in controlled studies.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR when adding or removing high-dose DHA (>2 g/day) in patients on warfarin. Alert prescriber to use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: DHA has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding DHA and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: DHA can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Dong quai contains coumarin derivatives (osthole, ferulic acid) that inhibit platelet aggregation. Case report documented >2-fold INR elevation in warfarin-stabilized patient after 4 weeks of dong quai.
drafts._source_action: Use caution with anticoagulant therapy. Monitor INR closely.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Dong quai has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding dong quai and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Dong quai can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: EPA reduces platelet TXA2 synthesis and may affect the vitamin K-dependent coagulation cascade. Clinical studies with high-dose fish oil (containing EPA) and warfarin show small but consistent INR elevations.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR when starting or stopping high-dose EPA (>2 g/day) in patients on warfarin or other anticoagulants.

fields_to_author.alert_body: EPA has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding EPA and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: EPA can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Parthenolide inhibits platelet aggregation via thromboxane synthase; additive bleeding risk with anticoagulant/antiplatelet therapy.
drafts._source_action: Monitor for increased bleeding tendency.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Feverfew has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding feverfew and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Feverfew can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Omega-3 fatty acids prolong bleeding time and may potentiate anticoagulant effects. FDA labels for icosapent ethyl and omega-3-acid ethyl esters explicitly state: 'omega-3 acids may prolong bleeding time. Monitor patients receiving anticoagulants.'
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR/bleeding time more frequently. Watch for signs of bruising or bleeding. Inform prescriber.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Fish oil has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding fish oil and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Fish oil can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Forskolin has demonstrated platelet aggregation inhibitory effects via cAMP-dependent suppression of platelet activation. This antiplatelet property may add to anticoagulant bleeding risk.
drafts._source_action: Monitor coagulation parameters when using forskolin with anticoagulants. Inform prescriber of supplement use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Forskolin has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding forskolin and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Forskolin can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Potential additive bleeding risk with anticoagulants.
drafts._source_action: Use with monitoring and clinician input.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Ginger has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding ginger and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Ginger can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Ginkgolide B inhibits PAF; co-administration with warfarin or DOACs increases hemorrhage risk. Case reports of subdural hematoma with ginkgo + warfarin.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR closely if ginkgo is used with anticoagulants. Consider avoiding combination.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Ginkgo has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding ginkgo and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Ginkgo can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Lion's mane (Hericium erinaceus) contains hericenones and erinacines that have demonstrated inhibition of ADP-induced and collagen-induced platelet aggregation in in vitro and animal studies, attributed to reduced thromboxane B2 synthesis. Combined with anticoagulant therapy, additive antiplatelet/anticoagulant effects may increase bleeding risk.
drafts._source_action: Monitor coagulation parameters when combining lion's mane with anticoagulants or antiplatelets. Report any unusual bruising or bleeding. Alert prescriber to supplement use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Lion's mane has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding lion's mane and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Lion's mane can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: NAC reduces disulfide bridges on coagulation factors and on platelet membrane glycoproteins, and it scavenges reactive oxygen species that normally promote pro-coagulant states. Combined with warfarin, case reports document enhanced anticoagulant effect and elevated INR.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR more frequently if starting or stopping NAC during anticoagulant therapy. Alert prescriber to any INR changes.

fields_to_author.alert_body: NAC has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding NAC and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: NAC can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Quercetin inhibits CYP2C9 (the primary metabolic enzyme for S-warfarin) and inhibits P-glycoprotein. Both mechanisms reduce warfarin clearance, potentially increasing plasma warfarin concentration and INR. Quercetin also inhibits platelet aggregation independently.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR frequently when starting or stopping quercetin supplementation in patients on warfarin. Alert prescriber to dose and any INR changes.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Quercetin has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding quercetin and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Quercetin can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Red clover's naturally occurring coumarin constituents (coumestrol) share structural similarity with vitamin K antagonists. While clinical evidence of warfarin potentiation is limited to case-level data, the herb is listed in systematic reviews of plants with anticoagulant potential. Additive effect with warfarin, heparins, or direct oral anticoagulants cannot be excluded.
drafts._source_action: Inform prescriber of red clover supplement use while on anticoagulant therapy. Consider periodic INR monitoring in warfarin users taking red clover supplements.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Red clover has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding red clover and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Red clover can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Resveratrol inhibits CYP2C9 (primary metabolism pathway for S-warfarin) and CYP3A4. Inhibition of CYP2C9 reduces warfarin clearance, raising plasma concentrations and INR. Concomitant antiplatelet activity adds further haemostatic impairment.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR closely when starting, adjusting, or stopping resveratrol in patients on warfarin. Alert prescriber; warfarin dose may need reduction.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Resveratrol has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding resveratrol and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Resveratrol can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Turmeric/curcumin may potentiate warfarin's anticoagulant effect through additive platelet inhibition (TXA2 suppression, COX-1 inhibition) and possible modulation of hepatic CYP2C9 metabolism of S-warfarin. The combination is listed as a potential warfarin-potentiating herb in systematic reviews covering 149 herb-warfarin interaction reports.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR when starting or increasing turmeric supplementation while on warfarin. Inform your prescriber. High-dose curcumin extracts (>500 mg curcumin/day) represent higher risk than culinary turmeric.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Turmeric has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding turmeric and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Turmeric can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Yerba mate contains vitamin K (phylloquinone) which may oppose the anticoagulant effect of vitamin K antagonists such as warfarin. Consistent intake of vitamin K-containing foods/supplements reduces INR. Additionally, caffeine may affect platelet aggregation. Clinically significant INR reduction has been reported with dietary vitamin K in warfarin patients.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR closely in patients on warfarin or other vitamin K antagonists who consume yerba mate regularly. Advise consistent intake rather than irregular use to minimize INR fluctuation.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Yerba mate has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding yerba mate and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Yerba mate can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Potential additive bleeding tendency.
drafts._source_action: Monitor with clinician guidance.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Ginger extract has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding ginger extract and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Ginger extract can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Theoretical concern based on COX-1 inhibition in platelets and 5-LOX inhibition that could additively suppress hemostatic mechanisms when combined with anticoagulants. No clinical case reports of warfarin-boswellia interaction have been identified. The AKBA-COX-1 interaction is mechanistically relevant but clinical INR impact is unconfirmed.
drafts._source_action: Inform your prescriber if using Boswellia supplements while on anticoagulant therapy. Monitor for unusual bruising or bleeding.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Boswellia has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding boswellia and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Boswellia can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Flaxseed's ALA-derived EPA may modestly affect platelet TXA2 production. The conversion rate from ALA to EPA in humans is limited (~5-10%), so anticoagulant potentiation is less likely than with direct EPA/DHA (fish oil) supplementation. No case reports of flaxseed altering warfarin INR have been identified.
drafts._source_action: Flaxseed supplementation is unlikely to require INR monitoring changes at typical doses. Inform prescriber of high-dose flaxseed oil use (>30 g ALA/day equivalent) during warfarin therapy.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Flaxseed has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding flaxseed and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Flaxseed can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: High-dose vitamin D (>10,000 IU/day) has been associated with altered warfarin sensitivity in some patients. The proposed mechanism involves shared metabolic pathways with vitamin K: vitamin D may modulate hepatic expression of vitamin K-dependent coagulation factors (II, VII, IX, X), and vitamin D deficiency itself affects coagulation factor synthesis. Vitamin D and K have documented synergistic effects on Gla-protein carboxylation, and disruption of this balance at supraphysiological vitamin D doses may affect INR stability in warfarin patients.
drafts._source_action: Monitor INR stability when significantly changing vitamin D supplementation dose, particularly at high doses (>10,000 IU/day). Routine supplementation doses (1000-4000 IU/day) typically do not require INR monitoring changes.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Vitamin D has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding Vitamin D and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Vitamin D can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Theoretical concern based on the case report of bleeding and proposed COX-mediated antiplatelet activity. No documented case reports of saw palmetto altering INR or warfarin activity. Risk is considered low at standard doses.
drafts._source_action: Inform your prescriber if taking saw palmetto while on anticoagulant therapy. Monitor for unusual bleeding or bruising.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Saw palmetto has mild antiplatelet or anticoagulant activity. If you take an anticoagulant, talk to your prescriber before adding saw palmetto and ask about INR or bleeding checks.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Saw palmetto can affect clotting — relevant to people on anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: AKBA directly and reversibly inhibits COX-1 in human platelets, the same enzyme targeted by aspirin (irreversibly) and NSAIDs. Combined use with antiplatelet agents produces mechanistically additive platelet inhibition through COX-1 suppression.
drafts._source_action: Monitor for signs of increased bleeding when combining Boswellia supplements with antiplatelet medications. Exercise caution with high-AKBA standardized extracts.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Boswellia has mild antiplatelet activity. If you take an antiplatelet medication like aspirin or clopidogrel, talk to your prescriber before adding boswellia.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Boswellia can affect platelet function — relevant to people on antiplatelets.

drafts._source_mechanism: Cat's claw extracts demonstrate antiplatelet activity in vitro. Procyanidins and other polyphenolic components inhibit platelet activation pathways. Combined with antiplatelet drugs, additive platelet inhibition is mechanistically predicted.
drafts._source_action: Monitor for signs of increased bleeding when combining cat's claw with antiplatelet medications.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Cat's claw has mild antiplatelet activity. If you take an antiplatelet medication like aspirin or clopidogrel, talk to your prescriber before adding cat's claw.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Cat's claw can affect platelet function — relevant to people on antiplatelets.

drafts._source_mechanism: Curcumin independently inhibits platelet aggregation via TXA2 suppression, COX-1, 5-LOX, and 12-LOX inhibition, and Ca2+ signaling blockade. Additive inhibition with antiplatelet drugs (aspirin, clopidogrel, ticagrelor) is mechanistically predicted and supported by in vitro data.
drafts._source_action: Monitor for signs of increased bleeding when combining curcumin supplements with antiplatelet medications. Avoid high-dose curcumin extracts (>1 g curcumin/day) without medical supervision in patients on dual antiplatelet therapy.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Curcumin has mild antiplatelet activity. If you take an antiplatelet medication like aspirin or clopidogrel, talk to your prescriber before adding curcumin.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Curcumin can affect platelet function — relevant to people on antiplatelets.

drafts._source_mechanism: Additive inhibition of platelet aggregation through independent TXA2 suppression. Combined use of high-dose fish oil with aspirin and warfarin has been associated with subdural hematoma after minor trauma.

drafts._source_mechanism: Boswellic acids (BAs), particularly acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (AKBA), inhibit COX-1 product formation in intact human platelets (IC50 ~6 µM) through direct reversible binding to the COX-1 enzyme. BAs are also potent specific inhibitors of 5-lipoxygenase (5-LO; IC50 1.5 µM for AKBA), reducing leukotriene B4 synthesis. This dual COX-1/5-LOX inhibition profile suggests meaningful antiplatelet potential at supplemental doses. Unlike NSAIDs, BAs do not inhibit the peroxidation of arachidonic acid, making them non-redox inhibitors.
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in patients with bleeding disorders. High-dose Boswellia extract standardized for AKBA may inhibit platelet function via COX-1. Consult a healthcare provider before use.

drafts._source_mechanism: Uncaria tomentosa (cat's claw) bark and leaf extracts demonstrate antiplatelet activity and thrombin inhibition in vitro. Ethanolic fractions inhibit thrombin with IC50 values of 5.86 µg/mL (amidolytic assay) and 12.48 µg/mL (proteolytic assay). The main active components responsible for thrombin inhibition are procyanidins B2 and C1, which interact with thrombin both within and outside the active site. Slight antiplatelet activity and prolonged thrombin time have also been demonstrated. Traditional use of cat's claw includes anticoagulant applications and hemorrhage therapy.
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in patients with bleeding disorders. Cat's claw extracts have demonstrated antiplatelet and thrombin-inhibiting activity in vitro. Consult a healthcare provider before use.

drafts._source_mechanism: Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) contains natural coumarins including herniarin and umbelliferone, which share structural similarity with warfarin. Additionally, chamomile contains flavonoids (apigenin, quercetin) that may inhibit platelet aggregation. A systematic review of herb-warfarin interactions identified chamomile tea as one of the herbs associated with bleeding events and INR elevation. A case of multiple thromboses and hemorrhage in a patient consuming chamomile tea (also taking warfarin) was reported.
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in patients with bleeding disorders. Concentrated chamomile extracts and large volumes of chamomile tea represent more risk than occasional use. Avoid in active bleeding.

drafts._source_mechanism: Curcumin preferentially inhibits platelet-activating factor (PAF)- and arachidonic acid (AA)-induced platelet aggregation with IC50 values of 20-25 µM. Mechanisms include: (1) inhibition of TXA2 synthesis (IC50 ~70 µM for TXA2 formation), (2) blockade of intracellular Ca2+ mobilization via the ionophore pathway, (3) inhibition of 12-lipoxygenase and 5-lipoxygenase, and (4) reduction of serotonin levels in platelets. Curcuminoids (the standardized extract) are approximately 2-fold more potent than curcumin alone in inhibiting platelet aggregation.
drafts._source_action: Avoid curcumin extracts in active bleeding disorders. Use with caution in patients with underlying coagulopathy or thrombocytopenia. Dietary turmeric at culinary doses is generally not a concern.

drafts._source_mechanism: Devil's claw (Harpagophytum procumbens) is documented in clinical pharmacology literature as having potential interactions with warfarin and possible anticoagulant activity. The proposed mechanisms involve iridoid glycoside harpagoside-mediated inhibition of arachidonic acid metabolism and modulation of COX/LOX pathways, which may contribute to antiplatelet effects. Devil's claw is explicitly listed as having documented reports of potential interactions with warfarin in the Heck et al. 2000 systematic review of alternative therapy-warfarin interactions (20 products reviewed).
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in patients with bleeding disorders. Devil's claw may affect platelet function and coagulation. Consult a healthcare provider before use.

drafts._source_mechanism: Evening primrose oil (EPO) contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), a precursor to dihomo-gamma-linolenic acid (DGLA) and prostaglandin E1 (PGE1). GLA shifts eicosanoid metabolism away from arachidonic acid-derived TXA2, with PGE1 inhibiting platelet aggregation. Animal studies show EPO significantly extends prothrombin time, activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT), and thrombin time, and decreases platelet count. EPO also demonstrated significant antiplatelet activity in a celecoxib-cardiovascular risk model.
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in patients with bleeding disorders or coagulopathy. High-dose EPO (>3 g/day) may affect coagulation parameters. Consult a healthcare provider before use.

drafts._source_action: Use with caution in patients with bleeding disorders. Doses above 3 g/day of EPA+DHA may significantly increase bleeding risk. Consult a healthcare provider before use.

In patients with pre-existing bleeding disorders, additive platelet inhibition may increase spontaneous bleeding risk.

TXA2 is the primary platelet aggregation amplifier; reduced TXA2 production measurably prolongs bleeding time at doses above 2 g/day.
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in bleeding disorders. Limit DHA to doses under 2 g/day unless under physician oversight. Inform surgeon of use before any procedure.

drafts._source_action: Use with caution in bleeding disorders. Limit EPA intake to under 2 g/day without physician oversight. Disclose use to any treating surgeon.

However, a randomized in vivo study of standard-dose saw palmetto (manufacturer's recommended dose, 2 weeks) found no measurable effect on PFA-100 platelet function assay in healthy adults.

The primary mechanisms are (1) inhibition of thromboxane A2 (TXA2) synthesis via COX-1 suppression, (2) inhibition of Ca2+ mobilization in platelets, and (3) suppression of 12-lipoxygenase and 5-lipoxygenase. At concentrated supplement doses, antiplatelet effects are clinically relevant.

drafts._source_mechanism: Cranberry (Vaccinium macrocarpon) contains flavonoids and proanthocyanidins that may inhibit CYP2C9, the primary enzyme responsible for S-warfarin metabolism. While in vitro data suggest CYP2C9 inhibitory potential, two prospective randomized controlled trials using metabolic surrogates found no significant CYP2C9 inhibition at typical cranberry juice consumption levels. Case reports of INR elevation exist but confounding factors were present. Direct antiplatelet effects from cranberry flavonoids are theoretically possible but not clinically established.
drafts._source_action: Cranberry at typical dietary amounts is unlikely to cause clinically significant bleeding. Use caution with concentrated cranberry supplements in patients with bleeding disorders, especially if also taking warfarin.

drafts._source_mechanism: Flaxseed is the richest plant source of alpha-linolenic acid (ALA, C18:3 n-3). ALA undergoes partial conversion to EPA in humans. Flaxseed oil supplementation significantly increases platelet EPA content (platelet EPA:AA ratio increases), which is associated with reduced TXA2 production and decreased platelet aggregation potential. In a human RCT, flaxseed oil supplementation (40 g/day for 23 days) reduced collagen-induced platelet aggregation at 0.75 and 2 µg collagen concentrations. However, at lower doses (10-40 g milled flaxseed/day in a 30-day RCT), no change in platelet aggregation or urinary thromboxane was observed, suggesting dose-dependence.
drafts._source_action: Flaxseed at typical dietary amounts (1-2 tablespoons/day) is generally safe. High-dose flaxseed oil supplementation may reduce platelet aggregation. Use caution in patients with active bleeding disorders. Consult a healthcare provider for high-dose use.

The bleeding risk in patients with bleeding disorders is primarily indirect — through potentiation of anticoagulant medications (warfarin), as documented in multiple case reports and FDA/WHO pharmacovigilance databases.
drafts._source_action: Glucosamine at standard doses (1500 mg/day) is not a direct bleeding risk for most patients with bleeding disorders. Use caution if also taking anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Vitamin D does not directly affect platelet aggregation at physiological or supplemental doses. However, high-dose vitamin D supplementation may interact with warfarin sensitivity in patients with concurrent vitamin K deficiency by affecting the vitamin K-dependent carboxylation of coagulation factors (II, VII, IX, X). The vitamin D endocrine system and vitamin K share overlapping roles in gamma-carboxylation of hepatic Gla-proteins.
drafts._source_action: Vitamin D at standard supplemental doses (up to 4000 IU/day) is not a clinically significant bleeding risk on its own. Monitor if taking high-dose vitamin D (>10,000 IU/day) with anticoagulants.

drafts._source_mechanism: Chondroitin sulfate is a glycosaminoglycan structurally similar to heparin. In vitro data suggest weak anticoagulant activity at high concentrations. This property is pharmacologically plausible but has not produced clinically documented bleeding events as a standalone effect. Patients with baseline bleeding disorders represent a population where even weak anticoagulant activity warrants monitoring.
drafts._source_action: Monitor for signs of increased bleeding in patients with bleeding disorders taking chondroitin supplements. No dose adjustment required in the absence of anticoagulant co-medication.

drafts._source_mechanism: Centella asiatica triterpenoids modulate collagen synthesis and vascular wall integrity. Preclinical data and the mechanism of wound-healing promotion via vascular remodeling raise a theoretical concern for altered hemostasis in patients with bleeding disorders or on anticoagulants. Patients with pre-existing heart disease, LV dysfunction, or on QT-prolonging medications face amplified arrhythmia risk.
drafts._source_action: Avoid regular licorice root use in heart disease, especially if combined with digoxin, loop diuretics, or QT-prolonging drugs. Monitor potassium and cardiac rhythm if use cannot be avoided.

drafts._source_mechanism: Calcium supplements (not dietary calcium) are associated with a modestly increased risk of myocardial infarction. Bolland et al. WHI reanalysis found RR 1.24 (95% CI 1.07–1.45) for MI with supplemental calcium. Proposed mechanism: acute hypercalcemia following bolus supplementation may promote coronary arterial calcification and thrombosis. Risk may be attenuated by co-administration with vitamin D.
drafts._source_action: In individuals with existing heart disease, prefer dietary calcium over supplements. If supplementation is necessary, use the lowest effective dose and split doses to blunt calcium spikes. Discuss with cardiologist.

drafts._source_mechanism: Guarana's high caffeine content (plus theobromine and theophylline) produces sympathomimetic cardiac stimulation: increased heart rate, elevated cardiac output, and potential for supraventricular and ventricular arrhythmias. In patients with pre-existing structural heart disease, heart failure, or arrhythmia history, these effects may destabilize cardiac rhythm or precipitate ischemia.
drafts._source_action: Avoid high-dose guarana supplements in patients with arrhythmia, ischemic heart disease, or heart failure. If caffeine-containing products are used, limit total daily caffeine to <200 mg/day.

PDE5 inhibition causes vasodilation; in patients with cardiovascular disease, particularly those on nitrates, this can precipitate severe hypotension. Icariin also has a weak estrogen receptor agonist activity.

drafts._source_mechanism: L-arginine is the substrate for nitric oxide synthase (NOS), producing NO which causes vasodilation. While this may benefit endothelial function in heart failure or coronary artery disease, the VINTAGE MI trial (post-MI patients) found unexpected excess deaths in the L-arginine group (6 vs 0 in placebo), leading to early termination. The mechanism may involve arrhythmogenic properties of NO at high concentrations in the post-infarction myocardium.
drafts._source_action: Avoid L-arginine supplementation in the immediate post-myocardial infarction period. Use with caution and only under cardiologist supervision in other heart disease settings.

drafts._source_mechanism: Heart failure patients are frequently treated with ACE inhibitors, ARBs, and/or aldosterone antagonists (spironolactone, eplerenone), all of which reduce renal potassium excretion. Adding supplemental potassium to these regimens substantially increases the risk of life-threatening hyperkalemia (>6.0 mEq/L), which can cause ventricular arrhythmias and cardiac arrest.
drafts._source_action: Do not take potassium supplements without physician approval and serum potassium monitoring if on ACE inhibitors, ARBs, or potassium-sparing diuretics. This is especially critical in heart failure management.

drafts._source_mechanism: Yerba mate contains caffeine and theobromine (xanthine stimulants) which increase heart rate and myocardial oxygen demand. Although isolated heart models demonstrate cardioprotective ischemia-reperfusion effects via Akt/eNOS pathways, the stimulant content poses risk for arrhythmia or increased cardiac workload in patients with existing heart disease (heart failure, arrhythmia, coronary artery disease).
drafts._source_action: Caution in patients with established heart disease, arrhythmias, or heart failure. Avoid high-dose yerba mate. Seek cardiologist guidance before regular use.

drafts._source_mechanism: Omega-3 fatty acids (EPA/DHA) have established cardioprotective effects, reducing major cardiovascular events (RR 0.95), MI (RR 0.90), and cardiovascular death (RR 0.94) in meta-analysis of 15 RCTs. However, high-dose prescription omega-3 supplementation (≥3g EPA+DHA/day) is associated with a significantly increased risk of atrial fibrillation (RR 1.25, 95% CI 1.10–1.41). This dose-dependent AFib risk is most prominent in populations with existing cardiovascular disease.
drafts._source_action: Low to moderate doses (≤2g/day) are generally cardioprotective. High doses (≥3g/day) should only be used under prescriber supervision with ECG monitoring for AFib in patients with heart disease or existing AFib risk factors.

drafts._source_mechanism: Vitamin D deficiency is associated with increased cardiovascular risk, and moderate supplementation is generally considered beneficial. However, pharmacologic doses (>4000 IU/day chronically) can cause hypercalcemia, which promotes vascular calcification and may worsen coronary artery disease. The benefit-risk ratio is dose-dependent.
drafts._source_action: Target serum 25(OH)D levels of 30–60 ng/mL. Avoid high-dose supplementation (>4000 IU/day) without clinical monitoring of calcium and 25(OH)D levels.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Vitamin D can affect heart rate or rhythm in some people. If you have heart disease, talk to your cardiologist before adding Vitamin D.

drafts._source_mechanism: CoQ10 (ubiquinone/ubiquinol) is involved in mitochondrial ATP synthesis in cardiac myocytes. Supplementation has shown modest benefit in heart failure (improved ejection fraction and symptom class in Q-SYMBIO trial). However, CoQ10 may interfere with anticoagulation therapy (warfarin), an important consideration in cardiac patients.
drafts._source_action: CoQ10 may be beneficial in heart failure under clinician guidance. Monitor INR closely if on warfarin, as CoQ10 has been associated with increased bleeding risk (OR 3.69 in longitudinal study). Discuss with cardiologist before initiating.

drafts._source_mechanism: Hawthorn (Crataegus) has positive inotropic and mild chronotropic effects via phosphodiesterase inhibition and increased calcium sensitivity. It is used as an adjunct in mild heart failure (NYHA Class I-II). The SPICE trial (post-LVEF <35%) did not show mortality benefit. Hawthorn may potentiate or interact with cardiac glycosides via additive effects on cardiac contractility and the potential for P-glycoprotein interactions affecting digoxin pharmacokinetics.
drafts._source_action: Hawthorn may be used as an adjunct in mild heart failure under physician supervision. Inform cardiologist if taking alongside digoxin or other cardiac medications. Monitor for bradycardia or hypotension.

drafts._source_mechanism: Magnesium plays a critical role in cardiac electrophysiology, stabilizing cardiac membranes and modulating ion channels. Hypomagnesemia is associated with cardiac arrhythmias. However, in patients with heart disease who also have renal impairment (common comorbidity), magnesium supplements can accumulate and cause hypermagnesemia, leading to bradycardia, cardiac conduction block, and potentially cardiac arrest.
drafts._source_action: Use magnesium supplements with caution in heart disease patients, especially those with reduced kidney function. Do not exceed recommended doses without renal function assessment. Monitor for signs of hypermagnesemia (weakness, bradycardia).

fields_to_author.alert_body: Magnesium can affect heart rate or rhythm in some people. If you have heart disease, talk to your cardiologist before adding magnesium.

drafts._source_mechanism: Caffeine causes acute blood pressure elevation (3-15 mmHg systolic) through adenosine receptor antagonism and sympathetic stimulation.
drafts._source_action: Limit caffeine-containing supplements. Monitor BP when starting caffeine supplements.

drafts._source_action: Avoid if taking calcium channel blockers or other CYP3A4-metabolized BP medications.

Reductions of 5-10 mmHg systolic have been reported in small trials, creating additive hypotension risk with antihypertensive therapy.
drafts._source_action: Monitor blood pressure when combining with antihypertensive medications. Alert prescriber to use.

drafts._source_action: Inform prescriber. Blood pressure may be less well-controlled. Dose adjustment may be needed.

drafts._source_action: Avoid taking green tea extract supplements close in time to beta-blockers. Separate doses by several hours.

drafts._source_mechanism: Guarana (Paullinia cupana) seeds contain 2-4.5% caffeine by dry weight — substantially higher than coffee beans (~1-2%). Caffeine causes acute blood pressure elevation (3-15 mmHg systolic) via adenosine receptor antagonism and sympathoadrenal activation. Guarana additionally contains theobromine and theophylline, which contribute to cardiovascular stimulation. Regular high-dose guarana supplementation can impair blood pressure control in hypertensive patients.
drafts._source_action: Limit guarana-containing supplements in hypertension. Monitor BP when initiating guarana supplementation. Account for total daily caffeine load from all sources.

drafts._source_mechanism: Yerba mate (Ilex paraguariensis) contains caffeine and theobromine, both xanthine stimulants with acute sympathomimetic effects on the cardiovascular system. Caffeine causes transient blood pressure elevation via catecholamine release and adenosine receptor antagonism. While one acute crossover RCT (n=13) found no significant BP effect at a single infusion dose, chronic or high-dose use may contribute to sustained hypertensive load, particularly in caffeine-sensitive individuals.
drafts._source_action: Patients with hypertension should limit or avoid yerba mate due to stimulant caffeine content. Monitor blood pressure. Avoid high-dose or frequent consumption.

Human evidence is limited but the mechanism supports a BP-lowering effect that could be additive with antihypertensive medications.

drafts._source_action: Monitor blood pressure when using tribulus alongside antihypertensive therapy. Report unusual dizziness or postural hypotension.

drafts._source_mechanism: Alpha-GPC (L-alpha glycerylphosphorylcholine) is a choline donor that directly increases brain acetylcholine synthesis and release by providing substrate for choline acetyltransferase. Anticholinergic drugs block muscarinic receptors to reduce cholinergic transmission. Alpha-GPC antagonises this pharmacological effect, potentially reducing the therapeutic efficacy of anticholinergics (e.g., overactive bladder treatment with oxybutynin) or creating unpredictable CNS effects.
drafts._source_action: Inform prescriber of alpha-GPC use if taking anticholinergic medications. The therapeutic effect of anticholinergics may be reduced. Monitor for loss of efficacy.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Alpha-GPC can influence cholinergic balance. If you take an anticholinergic medication, talk to your prescriber before adding Alpha-GPC.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Alpha-GPC affects cholinergic balance — relevant to people on anticholinergic medications.

drafts._source_mechanism: CBD inhibits CYP2C19 and CYP3A4, increasing plasma levels of clobazam, rufinamide, topiramate, and zonisamide. With valproate specifically, concomitant CBD causes ALT/AST elevation (hepatotoxicity risk). FDA drug labels for divalproex sodium explicitly warn about this interaction.
drafts._source_action: Monitor liver function tests if CBD used with valproate. Monitor anticonvulsant drug levels. Dose adjustment may be needed.

fields_to_author.alert_body: CBD can influence how seizure medications work. If you take an anticonvulsant, talk to your neurologist before adding CBD.
fields_to_author.informational_note: CBD may affect anticonvulsant therapy — relevant to people on seizure medications.

drafts._source_mechanism: CBD has CNS depressant effects and may potentiate sedation when combined with benzodiazepines, opioids, or other sedatives.
drafts._source_action: Use caution with concurrent sedative medications. Monitor for excessive drowsiness.

fields_to_author.alert_body: CBD has mild sedative effects. If you take a sedative or sleep medication, talk to your prescriber before adding CBD — the combination can increase drowsiness.

While apigenin shows weaker sedative activity than classical benzodiazepines, additive CNS depression is possible with concurrent sedative use.
drafts._source_action: Use caution when combining chamomile supplements (not chamomile tea) with prescription sedatives. Monitor for excessive drowsiness.

drafts._source_action: Advise caution when combining melatonin with CNS depressants. Avoid driving or operating machinery. Consider dose reduction of sedatives if concurrent use is necessary.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Melatonin has mild sedative effects. If you take a sedative or sleep medication, talk to your prescriber before adding melatonin — the combination can increase drowsiness.

drafts._source_action: Use caution when combining passionflower with prescription sedatives. Avoid driving or operating machinery. Reduce alcohol intake during concurrent use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Passionflower has mild sedative effects. If you take a sedative or sleep medication, talk to your prescriber before adding passionflower — the combination can increase drowsiness.

drafts._source_action: Avoid unsupervised combination with sedatives or antidepressants.

drafts._source_action: Advise patients taking CNS depressants or sedatives to use ashwagandha with caution and to monitor for excessive sedation.

drafts._source_action: Advise caution with concomitant use of sedative-hypnotic medications. Avoid if operating heavy machinery.

This CNS-active profile creates a pharmacodynamic additive risk with sedative or anxiolytic medications (benzodiazepines, barbiturates, sleep aids).
drafts._source_action: Advise caution when combining with prescription sedatives, benzodiazepines, or CNS depressants. Monitor for excessive sedation. Dose adjustment of the sedative may be warranted.

drafts._source_mechanism: Guarana's CNS stimulant effects (primarily via caffeine) pharmacodynamically oppose the action of sedative medications (benzodiazepines, barbiturates, non-benzodiazepine sleep aids, antihistamines with sedative properties). Combined use may impair the therapeutic efficacy of sedatives or, in opposite sequence, cause rebound stimulation that disrupts sleep architecture.
drafts._source_action: Avoid combining guarana supplements with sedative medications. If sedatives are prescribed for a medical reason, guarana and other high-caffeine supplements should be discontinued.

These CNS depressant effects may be additive with benzodiazepines, non-benzodiazepine hypnotics (z-drugs), and barbiturates.
drafts._source_action: Advise patients on sedative-hypnotic medications of potential additive sedation. Avoid high doses of L-theanine if operating machinery or driving.

drafts._source_action: Avoid combining valerian with prescription sedatives or sleep medications without physician oversight. Do not drive or operate machinery when combining these agents.

Additive CNS depression with sedatives, benzodiazepines, or sleep medications is a theoretical pharmacodynamic risk. CYP3A4 inhibition may also increase plasma levels of CYP3A4-metabolized benzodiazepines (e.g., midazolam, triazolam) — a combined pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic interaction.
drafts._source_action: Monitor for excessive sedation when combining with benzodiazepines or other sedatives. Note that CYP3A4 inhibition by schisandra can elevate levels of CYP3A4-metabolized sedatives.

Additionally, its adaptogenic/stimulant profile may interact unpredictably with CNS sedatives. Bidirectional modulation (stimulant at low dose, sedating at high dose) is reported in the literature.

drafts._source_mechanism: CBD has anticonvulsant properties (FDA-approved for Lennox-Gastaut and Dravet syndromes). However, CBD supplements are not pharmaceutical-grade and have variable potency, contaminant profiles, and dosing. Interaction with existing anticonvulsant regimens (especially valproate) may cause hepatotoxicity or alter drug levels.
drafts._source_action: Use only pharmaceutical-grade CBD (Epidiolex) for seizure management. Supplement-grade CBD should not be substituted. Inform neurologist of any CBD use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: CBD may affect seizure threshold in susceptible people. If you have a seizure disorder, talk to your neurologist before adding CBD.
fields_to_author.informational_note: CBD may affect seizure threshold — relevant to people with a seizure disorder.

drafts._source_mechanism: Borage seed oil contains high concentrations of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA, ~24%), similar to evening primrose oil. Borage also contains trace pyrrolizidine alkaloids in some preparations, which pose additional neurotoxic risk. Historical concern regarding GLA and seizure threshold lowering applies similarly to borage as to EPO.
drafts._source_action: Avoid high-dose borage oil in seizure disorders. If used, ensure products are certified pyrrolizidine alkaloid-free. Discuss with prescribing neurologist.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Borage seed oil may affect seizure threshold in susceptible people. If you have a seizure disorder, talk to your neurologist before adding borage seed oil.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Borage seed oil may affect seizure threshold — relevant to people with a seizure disorder.

drafts._source_mechanism: Evening primrose oil (EPO) contains gamma-linolenic acid (GLA). Concern regarding seizure threshold lowering originated from early case reports in the 1980s. A 2007 review concluded the association was likely spurious and that GLA and linoleic acid may actually have anticonvulsant properties. Risk is low but seizure disorder patients warrant monitoring and physician discussion.
drafts._source_action: Evening primrose oil is not absolutely contraindicated in seizure disorders based on current evidence, but patients with epilepsy should discuss use with their neurologist. Monitor for any changes in seizure control.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Evening primrose oil may affect seizure threshold in susceptible people. If you have a seizure disorder, talk to your neurologist before adding evening primrose oil.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Evening primrose oil may affect seizure threshold — relevant to people with a seizure disorder.

drafts._source_mechanism: Melatonin's effects on seizure threshold are debated. While some data suggests anticonvulsant properties via melatonin receptor MT1/MT2 modulation, case reports of seizure exacerbation in predisposed individuals exist. Overall risk is low but warrants physician discussion in active seizure disorders.
drafts._source_action: Use melatonin with caution in seizure disorders. Start at lowest effective dose and monitor for any changes in seizure frequency.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Melatonin may affect seizure threshold in susceptible people. If you have a seizure disorder, talk to your neurologist before adding melatonin.

drafts._source_mechanism: Ginkgo biloba seeds and insufficiently processed leaf products contain ginkgotoxin (4-O-methylpyridoxine), which inhibits GABA synthesis by depleting pyridoxal-5-phosphate cofactor, producing proconvulsant effects. Standardized 24% flavonoid / 6% terpene leaf extracts with ginkgotoxin removed are lower risk, but purity cannot be guaranteed from all manufacturers.
drafts._source_action: Use ginkgo with caution or avoid in seizure disorders. Use only standardized ginkgo leaf extract from reputable manufacturers. Monitor for increased seizure frequency.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Ginkgo may affect seizure threshold in susceptible people. If you have a seizure disorder, talk to your neurologist before adding ginkgo.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Ginkgo may affect seizure threshold — relevant to people with a seizure disorder.

drafts._source_mechanism: High-dose caffeine (the primary active compound in guarana) lowers the seizure threshold by antagonizing adenosine receptors, which normally exert inhibitory control over neuronal excitability. Case reports document caffeine-triggered seizures in susceptible individuals, and animal studies confirm proconvulsant effects at high doses. Standard-dose guarana supplements provide caffeine equivalent to 1-4 cups of coffee.
drafts._source_action: Use caution with guarana in patients with seizure disorders or epilepsy. Limit total daily caffeine from all sources. Inform neurologist of guarana supplement use. Avoid energy drink and pre-workout products containing guarana.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Guarana may affect seizure threshold in susceptible people. If you have a seizure disorder, talk to your neurologist before adding guarana.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Guarana may affect seizure threshold — relevant to people with a seizure disorder.

drafts._source_mechanism: Huperzine A inhibits acetylcholinesterase (AChE), increasing synaptic acetylcholine. Elevated cholinergic tone in the CNS can lower seizure threshold and has been associated with seizure-like activity in overdose scenarios. The clinical significance at standard supplement doses is unclear but warrants caution.
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in seizure disorders. Alert neurologist to huperzine A use. Report any changes in seizure frequency.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Huperzine A may affect seizure threshold in susceptible people. If you have a seizure disorder, talk to your neurologist before adding Huperzine A.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Huperzine A may affect seizure threshold — relevant to people with a seizure disorder.

drafts._source_mechanism: Kavalactones have demonstrated anticonvulsant properties in animal models via GABA-A receptor modulation. However, abrupt discontinuation after regular use may produce withdrawal seizures. Additionally, unpredictable CNS effects make kava use in epilepsy patients risky without neurologist oversight.
drafts._source_action: Avoid kava in seizure disorders without neurologist supervision. Do not abruptly discontinue if already in use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Kava may affect seizure threshold in susceptible people. If you have a seizure disorder, talk to your neurologist before adding kava.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Kava may affect seizure threshold — relevant to people with a seizure disorder.

drafts._source_mechanism: Soy isoflavones including genistein inhibit thyroid peroxidase (TPO), the enzyme required for thyroid hormone synthesis. Peripubertal and high-dose exposure associated with subclinical hypothyroidism via disruption of the hypothalamic-pituitary-thyroid axis.
drafts._source_action: Individuals with thyroid disorders should discuss soy isoflavone supplementation with their healthcare provider. Monitor thyroid function tests if using high-dose soy isoflavone supplements.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Genistein can influence thyroid hormone levels. If you have a thyroid condition, talk to your endocrinologist before adding genistein and monitor your labs.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Genistein affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people with thyroid conditions.

drafts._source_mechanism: L-carnitine (including the acetylated form ALCAR) has been shown in a placebo-controlled trial to act as a peripheral antagonist of thyroid hormone action by preventing thyroid hormone nuclear translocation. It may thus reduce the effectiveness of exogenous thyroid hormone therapy and potentially worsen hypothyroidism at high doses.
drafts._source_action: Monitor TSH in patients with hypothyroidism taking ALCAR. Alert endocrinologist to supplement use; dose of levothyroxine may need adjustment.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Acetyl-L-carnitine can influence thyroid hormone levels. If you have a thyroid condition, talk to your endocrinologist before adding Acetyl-L-carnitine and monitor your labs.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Acetyl-L-carnitine affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people with thyroid conditions.

drafts._source_mechanism: Ashwagandha (Withania somnifera) root extract increases serum T3 and T4 levels and decreases TSH, with pro-thyroid effects demonstrated in both animal hypothyroidism models and human endocrine reviews. This can alter thyroid function test results and may be inappropriate in hyperthyroid states.
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in thyroid disorders. Monitor thyroid function tests (TSH, T3, T4) during use. Contraindicated in hyperthyroidism without physician oversight.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Ashwagandha can influence thyroid hormone levels. If you have a thyroid condition, talk to your endocrinologist before adding ashwagandha and monitor your labs.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Ashwagandha affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people with thyroid conditions.

drafts._source_mechanism: Bacopa monnieri has been shown in animal studies to increase thyroid hormone levels (T4 specifically) via stimulation of thyroid iodide uptake and thyroglobulin synthesis. The clinical relevance in humans is uncertain but persons with hyperthyroidism or Graves disease may be at greater risk.
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in thyroid disorders, particularly hyperthyroidism. Monitor thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4) periodically during use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Bacopa can influence thyroid hormone levels. If you have a thyroid condition, talk to your endocrinologist before adding bacopa and monitor your labs.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Bacopa affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people with thyroid conditions.

drafts._source_mechanism: Excess iodine triggers the Wolff-Chaikoff effect, acutely suppressing thyroid hormone synthesis. Individuals with pre-existing thyroid disease (Hashimoto thyroiditis, Graves disease) are particularly susceptible to iodine-induced hypothyroidism or hyperthyroidism. Kelp/seaweed products contain highly variable iodine loads (50–500% of stated dose) and carry additional risk.
drafts._source_action: Avoid high-dose iodine or kelp/seaweed supplements in thyroid disorders without physician oversight. Routine supplementation above the RDA (150 mcg/day) is not recommended.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Iodine can influence thyroid hormone levels. If you have a thyroid condition, talk to your endocrinologist before adding iodine and monitor your labs.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Iodine affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people with thyroid conditions.

drafts._source_mechanism: L-tyrosine is the direct biosynthetic precursor to thyroid hormones (T3 and T4). Thyroglobulin contains tyrosine residues that are iodinated to form monoiodotyrosine and diiodotyrosine, which couple to form T3 and T4. Supplemental tyrosine loading may theoretically increase thyroid hormone synthesis, particularly concerning in hyperthyroidism or Graves disease.
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in hyperthyroidism. Monitor thyroid function tests (TSH, free T4) when initiating L-tyrosine supplementation. Alert endocrinologist to use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: L-tyrosine can influence thyroid hormone levels. If you have a thyroid condition, talk to your endocrinologist before adding L-tyrosine and monitor your labs.
fields_to_author.informational_note: L-tyrosine affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people with thyroid conditions.

drafts._source_mechanism: Quercetin inhibits thyroid peroxidase (TPO), the enzyme responsible for iodinating tyrosine residues in thyroglobulin to form T3 and T4. At high supplemental doses (>500 mg/day), this TPO inhibition may impair thyroid hormone synthesis, contributing to goitre formation or worsening hypothyroidism, particularly in individuals with borderline iodine intake.
drafts._source_action: Use with caution in thyroid disorders, especially hypothyroidism. Limit dose to under 500 mg/day. Monitor TSH periodically and inform endocrinologist of use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Quercetin can influence thyroid hormone levels. If you have a thyroid condition, talk to your endocrinologist before adding quercetin and monitor your labs.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Quercetin affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people with thyroid conditions.

drafts._source_mechanism: The soy isoflavones genistein and daidzein — present in red clover at significant concentrations — are established inhibitors of thyroid peroxidase (TPO), the enzyme responsible for thyroid hormone synthesis. In vitro data demonstrate that genistein and daidzein competitively inhibit TPO at nutritional concentrations. In iodine-deficient individuals or those on thyroid replacement therapy, this inhibition can impair thyroid hormone synthesis and reduce circulating T3/T4.
drafts._source_action: Use caution in patients with hypothyroidism or those on levothyroxine. Separate red clover isoflavone supplements from thyroid medications by at least 4 hours. Monitor thyroid function (TSH) if initiating high-dose red clover supplements.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Red clover can influence thyroid hormone levels. If you have a thyroid condition, talk to your endocrinologist before adding red clover and monitor your labs.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Red clover affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people with thyroid conditions.

drafts._source_mechanism: Selenium is essential for iodothyronine deiodinase (type I/II/III) function required to convert T4 to active T3. Selenium supplementation may benefit Hashimoto thyroiditis by reducing thyroid peroxidase antibody titers. However, excess selenium (>400 mcg/day) can paradoxically impair thyroid function and is toxic.
drafts._source_action: Selenium supplementation in thyroid disorders should be guided by serum selenium levels. Doses above 200 mcg/day should only be used under medical supervision.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Selenium can influence thyroid hormone levels. If you have a thyroid condition, talk to your endocrinologist before adding selenium and monitor your labs.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Selenium affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people with thyroid conditions.

drafts._source_mechanism: High-dose biotin (>5 mg/day, common in hair/nail supplements) interferes with biotin-streptavidin immunoassay platforms used for TSH, free T4, free T3, and thyroglobulin measurement. Causes false-low TSH and false-high free thyroid hormone results, mimicking hyperthyroidism. Does not alter actual thyroid function.
drafts._source_action: Stop biotin supplements at least 2 days before thyroid function testing to prevent assay interference. Inform your healthcare provider about biotin use if thyroid tests are planned.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Biotin can influence thyroid hormone levels. If you have a thyroid condition, talk to your endocrinologist before adding Biotin and monitor your labs.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Biotin affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people with thyroid conditions.

drafts._source_mechanism: Soy isoflavones can inhibit levothyroxine absorption when taken simultaneously and may independently suppress thyroid hormone synthesis via TPO inhibition, requiring dose adjustments.
drafts._source_action: Separate soy isoflavone supplements from levothyroxine by at least 4 hours. Monitor TSH levels.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Genistein can interact with thyroid hormone absorption or metabolism. If you take thyroid medication, talk to your endocrinologist about timing genistein away from your dose.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Genistein affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people on thyroid medication.

drafts._source_action: Monitor TSH levels when starting or stopping ALCAR in patients on levothyroxine or other thyroid hormone replacement therapies.

fields_to_author.informational_note: Acetyl-L-carnitine affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people on thyroid medication.

drafts._source_action: Monitor TSH levels when initiating or discontinuing Bacopa in patients on thyroid hormone replacement. Alert prescriber to supplement use.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Bacopa can interact with thyroid hormone absorption or metabolism. If you take thyroid medication, talk to your endocrinologist about timing bacopa away from your dose.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Bacopa affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people on thyroid medication.

drafts._source_mechanism: Calcium forms insoluble chelate complexes with levothyroxine in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing oral absorption by 20–25%. Effect is dose-dependent and clinically significant at standard supplementation doses (600–1000 mg/day).
drafts._source_action: Separate calcium supplementation from levothyroxine by at least 4 hours to prevent absorption interference.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Calcium can interact with thyroid hormone absorption or metabolism. If you take thyroid medication, talk to your endocrinologist about timing calcium away from your dose.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Calcium affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people on thyroid medication.

drafts._source_mechanism: Chromium picolinate can reduce levothyroxine absorption when taken concurrently. Divalent chromium binds to thyroid hormone molecules in the gastrointestinal tract.
drafts._source_action: Separate chromium supplementation from levothyroxine by at least 4 hours.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Chromium can interact with thyroid hormone absorption or metabolism. If you take thyroid medication, talk to your endocrinologist about timing chromium away from your dose.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Chromium affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people on thyroid medication.

drafts._source_mechanism: High dietary fiber intake adsorbs levothyroxine in the gut, reducing absorption. Psyllium and other supplemental fibers taken simultaneously with thyroid medication can significantly impair bioavailability.
drafts._source_action: Separate fiber supplements from levothyroxine by at least 4 hours.

fields_to_author.alert_body: Fiber can interact with thyroid hormone absorption or metabolism. If you take thyroid medication, talk to your endocrinologist about timing fiber away from your dose.
fields_to_author.informational_note: Fiber affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people on thyroid medication.

drafts._source_action: Separate iron supplementation from levothyroxine by at least 4 hours.

fields_to_author.informational_note: Iron affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people on thyroid medication.

drafts._source_action: Monitor TSH levels periodically if taking L-tyrosine with levothyroxine. Alert endocrinologist to supplement use.

fields_to_author.informational_note: L-tyrosine affects thyroid pathways — relevant to people on thyroid medication.

drafts._source_action: Separate magnesium supplementation from levothyroxine by at least 4 hours.

drafts._source_action: Separate zinc supplementation from levothyroxine by at least 4 hours.

drafts._source_mechanism: High iodine intake can alter thyroid physiology and therapy response.
drafts._source_action: Coordinate iodine supplements with thyroid medication management.
